summary: Stealth-Keyword Auto-Promote — fully verified end-to-end on live preview. Backend: 20/20 unit tests + 14/14 live HTTP tests = 34/34 green. Frontend: AP block renders with all 7 data-testids (block, enabled, min-impressions, save-min, history, row-{id}, undo-{id}). Verified: GET digest/settings returns auto_promote_enabled+auto_promote_min_impressions; PUT round-trip + clamping (high→500, low→5, garbage→422 or unchanged); admin history returns rows[] with raw token redacted (only token_hint), 422 on out-of-range limit; admin undo by id 404 on unknown record; public token-undo route is anon-accessible and 404s on bogus token. UI: checkbox toggle false→true→false works, min-impressions input correctly conditional on enabled state, save-min PUTs new threshold, 'Auto-promote paused' toast confirmed on disable. History container hidden when rows=0 (expected on live). DB left production-safe: auto_promote_enabled=false, min_impressions=20.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/stealth_seo.py admin_undo_auto_promote does an extra DB lookup to fetch the token by record_id, then calls undo_by_token which fetches the same row again — could be folded into a single undo_by_record_id() helper to halve the round-trips. Functional but slightly wasteful.

services/stealth_seo_auto_promote.py apply_auto_promote stamps admin_email='auto-promote@cron' (a non-email-shaped string) into set_collection_keywords. If downstream audit/email pipelines validate this field as RFC-5322, this could fail silently — recommend either a real email shape (e.g. cron@auto-promote.local) or a separate `actor` field that doesn't pretend to be an email.

services/stealth_seo_auto_promote.py undo_by_token does NOT verify the keyword is still present before removing — if an admin manually deleted the keyword between promote and undo, the undo silently succeeds with empty work. Acceptable, but logging a 'kw_already_removed' flag in the response would help debugging.

list_recent uses a 60-day cutoff but the digest pulls trailing 8 days — if a promotion is undone after 8 days the digest won't reflect it, but list_recent (admin history view) will. Two different time windows is correct intentionally, just worth a comment.

stealth_seo_auto_promote.py imports services.stealth_seo lazily inside 4 functions (get_collection_keywords / set_collection_keywords). Lazy import is acceptable to break circular imports but a single module-level import inside a TYPE_CHECKING-style guard would be cleaner.

Public undo endpoint returns identical HTML for valid + already_undone — only differs by the parenthetical '(already undone)' in the heading. Consider adding a structured response (JSON ?format=json) for ops dashboards to programmatically confirm undo state.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_stealth_seo_auto_promote_live.py (NEW — 14 live HTTP tests covering RBAC + schema + clamping + redaction + 404 paths)
seed_data_creation: No real promotions triggered. Settings PUT/round-trip used during tests; auto_promote_enabled briefly flipped true during UI test then restored to false. min_impressions left at 20 (default). DB left production-safe as instructed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-promote feature shipped clean. Live HTTP tests at /app/backend/tests/test_stealth_seo_auto_promote_live.py (~5s, idempotent, restores DB state). Unit tests at /app/backend/tests/test_stealth_seo_auto_promote.py exercise the full promote+undo path with mocked DB+collections. UI selectors: stealth-perf-open-btn → wait stealth-perf-kpis → stealth-ap-{block,enabled,min-impressions,save-min,history,row-{id},undo-{id}}. Min-impressions input + save button are conditional on stealth-ap-enabled being checked. History container only renders when rows.length > 0 (live preview = 0 rows → container absent, which is expected). On preview DB: digest enabled=true, recipients=[], auto_promote_enabled=false, auto_promote_min_impressions=20.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Auto-promote backend + frontend are production-ready and DB left in a safe state.
